summary:
Chief cells are primarily found in the base of gastric glands within the oxyntic region, particularly concentrated in the fundus but also present in the body. The fundamental distinction lies in their specific position—within the basal portion of gastric glands—not by regional distribution across all areas equally.\boxed{\text{A}}
